head: d91977d
sessionMd:
# SESSION.md — beatgate build notes

## Runs before green

- `npm run check`: **green on the 1st run.**
- `npm test`: green on the **2nd** run. The one failure was my own test's
  float naivety (`0.55 - 0.5 > 0.05` in binary), not engine or game logic;
  fixed by using binary-exact edge values (0.0625 halves).
- `npm run verify`: green on the **2nd** run. First run (with
  `UPDATE_GOLDEN=1`) had 3 failures:
  1–2. "confirm restarts on the win/lose screen" — the scripted run's final
  scoring press was still *held* when I sent the restart press, so there was
  no rising edge. Fixed with an explicit release frame. (This exact trap is
  documented — FUN.md Part 3: "bot-driver inputs release between edges" — I
  read it and still walked into it; the docs are fine, the trap is just real.)
  3. My time-to-first-hit feel window (0.3–5s) was invented before measuring;
  seed 1 places the first gate near the sweep start, so the first hit lands at
  0.2s. Retuned the lower bound to the start-grace length (the earliest a hit
  is even possible), which is a derived bound rather than a vibed one.

## `@hayao` names that turned out not to exist

None. I grepped `docs/API.md` before writing any import, per AGENTS.md, and
every name used (`defineGame`, `createWorld`, `Node`, `Sprite`, `Text`,
`audio`, `showScreen`/`hideScreen`, `registerNode`, `checkDeterministic`,
`assertSnapshotStable`, `recordTimeline`, `firstFrame`, `inputDensity`,
`longestLull`, `changeFrames`, `renderFilmstrip`, `runBrowser`,
`DEFAULT_INPUT_MAP`-shaped `InputMap`) existed with the documented signature.

## Where the docs were wrong, missing, or misleading

1. **VERIFICATION.md "The gate" section is stale.** It says "Wire your game's
   puzzle + game into `scripts/verify.ts`" — but `scripts/verify.ts` is an
   auto-discovering runner (and CONVENTIONS.md forbids touching `scripts/`).
   The real mechanism (ship `examples/<slug>/verify.ts` with a default-export
   `verify(t)`) is documented only in a comment at the top of
   `scripts/verify.ts` itself.
2. **No documented recipe for a title screen, and no example ships one.** The
   spec's "standard title / game-over overlays" implies a standard; in the
   repo, all 21 examples start straight in gameplay and use `showScreen` only
   for win/lose. I had to design the title phase fresh: a `mode: 'title'` in
   the pure state so the loop is sim-driven and verifiable headlessly, with
   the DOM overlay as a projection of it.
3. **Undocumented double-fire between overlays and the input map.** The
   overlay's built-in keyboard handler consumes Enter/Space to trigger
   `onSelect`, but the same keydown also reaches the input system as a
   `confirm` edge on the next step — so dismissing a game-over screen with
   Enter would instantly score a miss in the fresh run. No doc mentions this.
   I absorbed it with `START_GRACE` frames (confirm swallowed for 12 frames
   after a (re)start), which also protects double-taps; worth a line in
   CONVENTIONS or a fix in `overlay.ts` (stopPropagation on handled keys).
4. **`assertSnapshotStable` doesn't assert.** Despite the `assert*` name it
   returns `{ ok, hashA, hashB }` and never throws (API.md shows the
   signature, so no harm done — but the name promises `assertSolvable`
   semantics it doesn't have).
5. Minor: `renderFilmstrip` captures frame 0 before the view's first
   `onProcess`, so any shape set in `redraw()` rather than construction shows
   its constructor default in the first panel. Cosmetic only; invisible in
   the browser (first rAF step runs before paint).
